pi_items.source: Comparison of setup_task.sh JSON with CPSC.gov: Hazard description (cord bushing, fire/burn/shock) matches Travel Steam Irons. Model numbers (SF-425, SF-435, SF-440, SF-445, SF-447) match handheld steamers. Units recalled (2M + 13K) match handheld steamers, not travel irons (~317K US + 5K Canada).

pi_items.metadata_value: About 2 million (In addition, about 13,000 were sold in Canada)
pi_items.verified_value: If real travel irons: ~317,000 US + ~5,000 Canada. If handheld steamers: ~2,000,000 US + ~13,000 Canada. Metadata matches handheld steamer numbers.
pi_items.source: CPSC.gov: April 27, 2023 Travel Steam Iron recall = 317K US + 5K Canada. February 2024 Handheld Steamer recall = 2M US + 13K Canada.

pi_items.metadata_value: The power cord can become damaged near the cord bushing, posing fire, burn and shock hazards. The cord bushing can also overheat, posing a fire and burn hazard.
pi_items.verified_value: This hazard description matches the April 27, 2023 Travel Steam Iron recall (SF-717, SF-720, SF-727), not the handheld steamer recall.
pi_items.source: CPSC.gov April 2023 recall page: 'power cord can become damaged near the cord bushing...fire, burn and shock hazards'
Multiple CPSC recall pages and Steamfast.com confirm this is the correct Vornado Recall Team phone number

pi_items.verified_value: Real April 2023 recall: 50 reports. Real handheld steamer recall: 122 reports. Neither matches 74.
pi_items.source: CPSC.gov: Travel Steam Irons had 50 total incidents (38 smoking/sparking/burning, 12 cord damage). Handheld steamers had 122 incidents (hot water spraying).

privileged_info_summary: This task uses a synthetic dataset that combines elements from real CPSC recalls. The product is described as 'Steamfast Travel Steam Irons' with a hazard description matching the April 27, 2023 CPSC recall (cord bushing damage causing fire, burn, shock hazards). However, the model numbers listed (SF-425, SF-430, SF-432, SF-435, SF-437, SF-438, SF-439, SF-440, SF-445, SF-447) do not match real travel iron models (SF-717, SF-720, SF-727). Five of the model numbers (SF-425, SF-435, SF-440, SF-445, SF-447) are from a different 2024 handheld garment steamer recall. Five model numbers (SF-430, SF-432, SF-437, SF-438, SF-439) do not exist in any CPSC database. The units recalled figure (2 million + 13,000) matches the handheld steamer recall, not the travel iron recall. The phone number 866-827-3362 is verified as the correct Vornado/Steamfast recall customer service number. The consumer contact information references the correct website (steamfast.com/recalls/travelirons). The task requires users to create a CPSC-compliant press release document based on this synthetic JSON data.
